seo.meta_title: WorkOS vs Logto for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins?
seo.meta_description: Detailed WorkOS vs Logto comparison for Next.js App Router. See how they stack up on pricing, developer experience, and real-world trade-offs.
seo.og_title: WorkOS or Logto for Next.js? Our Honest Comparison
seo.og_description: WorkOS (4★) vs Logto (4.1★). Advanced learning curve vs Medium. Find your ideal Next.js auth solution.

hero.headline: WorkOS vs Logto for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of WorkOS and Logto — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: WorkOS vs Logto is a common comparison we hear from Next.js developers. WorkOS (4★) and Logto (4.1★) both have loyal followings. Our analysis shows Logto has the edge for most Next.js projects due to its 7/10 DX score. WorkOS is a strong alternative if no free tier (enterprise sales) better matches your budget.
verdict_text: For most Next.js developers building production apps, we recommend Logto. It offers better Next.js integration (7/10 vs 5/10), a medium learning curve, and a strong feature set that aligns well with App Router patterns. WorkOS remains a solid choice — especially if no free tier (enterprise sales) is important to your stack.

pros: Enterprise-grade SSO with SAML/OIDC support out of the box
SCIM provisioning for enterprise directory integration
SOC 2 compliant and enterprise-ready from day one
Clean API design with excellent documentation
Used by Vercel, Airbnb, and other top-tier tech companies
cons: No free tier - requires sales conversation to get started
Not designed for consumer auth or B2C applications
Pricing is opaque and requires a sales call
Overkill for teams that don't need enterprise SSO

pros: Built-in management API and admin console
Support for social sign-in, MFA, and passwordless auth
User-friendly admin dashboard for non-technical team members
cons: Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Next.js SDK is less mature with fewer examples
Self-hosted version requires significant infrastructure setup

faq.question: Should I choose WorkOS or Logto for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. WorkOS scores 5/10 for Next.js integration with a Advanced learning curve. Logto scores 7/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Logto because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if No free tier (enterprise sales) or Yes, up to 5,000 MAU is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is WorkOS or Logto more affordable?
faq.answer: WorkOS starts at Custom pricing (enterprise) with a free tier of No free tier (enterprise sales). Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Logto offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is WorkOS or Logto easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve with an estimated setup time of 45 minutes. Logto has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Logto is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use WorkOS and Logto together?
faq.answer: Using both WorkOS and Logto in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: WorkOS or Logto?
faq.answer: WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 5/10, and a user rating of 4★. Logto has a Medium learning curve, 7/10, and 4.1★. On paper, Logto leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
